title: Speculation Willes public pretext is restoring the ocean
display_title: Speculation Willes public pretext is restoring the ocean
wikitext:
[]: [Speculation] Wille´s public pretext is restoring the ocean?             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Fri Jun 28, 2013 10:14 am I´ve read sometimes that Wille´s public pretext is that they are restoring the ocean from red to blue. Is that true? They don´t mention it in the film. It would make sense, Kaji seemed to know people that worked in that facility in 2.22. And do we know what the red liquid in the ocean is? Blood? LCL? And is it still Lilith´s blood? We see Lilith exploding into LCL, but if that liquid in the ocean is LCL it can´t come only from Lilith. And she didn´t participate in Second Impact.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Jun 28, 2013 10:20 am The bit about the pretext for Wille came from an erroneous post shortly after Q dropped, and indeed has caught me out since! There's a discussion of it somewhere, but I don't have time to find it right now - suffice it to say that there is no basis for it.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Fri Jun 28, 2013 11:06 am I think the theory was that Wille was the organization behind the giant ocean-filtering complex that the kids visited with Kaji in 2.0.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Fri Jun 28, 2013 11:48 am This should really be relabeled as speculation.

[]:             Quote Post by Charsi » Fri Jun 28, 2013 1:02 pm Conjecture based on fanwankery/rumor that grew out of early screening reports. Turned out to have no supporting evidence, but still kind of a cool bit of fanwankery.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Fri Jun 28, 2013 1:16 pm It's just fan-wankery that was made up immediately after the theatrical release. It's up there with Mari being revealed as Shinji & Asuka's time-traveling daughter - another "plot point" that underwent serious discussions around the Internet.

[]: Re: Wille´s public pretext is restoring the ocean?             Quote Post by gchristnsn » Fri Jun 28, 2013 1:28 pm kuribo-04 wrote: And do we know what the red liquid in the ocean is? Blood? LCL? And is it still Lilith´s blood? We see Lilith exploding into LCL, but if that liquid in the ocean is LCL it can´t come only from Lilith. And she didn´t participate in Second Impact.

[]: Re: Wille´s public pretext is restoring the ocean?             Quote Post by Jurrasic » Fri Jun 28, 2013 3:06 pm gchristnsn wrote: There is the moment at the first movie where Maya compares the lake of blood from angel with the second impact. Kaji outlines the bloody ocean as the result of the 2I. This means that this is blood. My theory: there also was that big meaty thing with teeth and eye at the South Pole, and it exploded filling the ocean with blood.

[]: Re: Wille´s public pretext is restoring the ocean?             Quote Post by gchristnsn » Fri Jun 28, 2013 3:12 pm ^ A nice speculation, I think, is that this is weird alien Adam's blood. If it would be LCL (primordial soup) life would bloom in it.

[]: Re: Wille´s public pretext is restoring the ocean?             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Fri Jun 28, 2013 4:36 pm gchristnsn wrote: A nice speculation, I think, is that this is weird alien Adam's blood. If it would be LCL (primordial soup) life would bloom in it.

[]: Re: Wille´s public pretext is restoring the ocean?             Quote Post by gchristnsn » Fri Jun 28, 2013 5:05 pm riffraff11235 wrote: Not on a timescale of 30 years. It took quite some time for even bacteria to form from all the chemicals in our primordial oceans. You'd probably only find small chains of nitrogenous bases at most in that red ocean.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Jun 28, 2013 5:46 pm The oceans seems saturated with LCL, look at what's written bellow the red screen in the Aquarium decontamination facility : there is a "L.C.L. Administrative area" inscription : SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by gchristnsn » Fri Jun 28, 2013 5:52 pm ElMariachi wrote: The oceans seems saturated with LCL, look at what's written bellow the red screen in the Aquarium decontamination facility : there is a "L.C.L. Administrative area" inscription :

[]:             Quote Post by wiser3754 » Fri Jun 28, 2013 6:37 pm Forget the ocean, what about the moon? It's that close to collision that it would have far more devastating consequences than 2nd, 3rd and fourth Impact combined.
